mission.title: dragonfly: Add support for Pub/Sub ACLs
mission.description: Add support for Pub/Sub ACLs. Requested by 20+ users.

steps.title: Check current dragonfly deployment
steps.description:
Verify your dragonfly version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n dragonfly-system -l app.kubernetes.io/name=dragonfly
helm list -n dragonfly-system 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working dragonfly installation.

steps.title: Review dragonfly configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant dragonfly configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n dragonfly-system -l app.kubernetes.io/name=dragonfly
kubectl get configmap -n dragonfly-system -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=dragonfly
```
**Did you search GitHub Issues and GitHub Discussions First?**
Yes

We are implementing Dragonfly for a multi-tenant application.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Add support for Pub/Sub ACLs
steps.description:
This PR adds support for `pub/sub` ACL's. After it is merged the feature is feature complete :)

* add support for pub/sub
* add tests

See the source issue for community-verified solutions.

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n dragonfly-system -l app.kubernetes.io/name=dragonfly
kubectl get events -n dragonfly-system --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Add support for Pub/Sub ACLs" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
This PR adds support for `pub/sub` ACL's. After it is merged the feature is feature complete :)

* add support for pub/sub
* add tests

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with dragonfly installed or the issue environment reproducible.
